description:
To properly set the permissions of /etc/ipsec.d, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0700 /etc/ipsec.d
rationale:
Setting correct permissions on the /etc/ipsec.d directory is important
because this directory hosts Libreswan configuration. Protection of this
directory is critical for system security. Restricting the permissions
ensures exclusive control of the Libreswan configuration.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /etc/ipsec.d, run the command: 
$ ls -l /etc/ipsec.d
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: 0700
fixtext:
Configure the "/etc/ipsec.d" file to "0700" by running the following command:
$ sudo chmod 0700 /etc/ipsec.d
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/ipsec.d file must have mode 0700 or less permissive to prevent unauthorized access.
title: Verify Permissions On /etc/ipsec.d Directory
